trans-unit.source: A Phase 1, Open-label, Dose-escalating Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of ALXN1850 in Adults with Hypophosphatasia
trans-unit.target: A Phase 1, Open-label, Dose-escalating Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of ALXN1850 in Adults with Hypophosphatasia

trans-unit.source: Number of Study Center(s) and Countries:
trans-unit.target: Number of Study Center(s) and Countries:

trans-unit.source: This study was conducted at 2 centers in the United States; however, 1 center did not enroll participants.
trans-unit.target: This study was conducted at 2 centers in the United States; however, 1 center did not enroll participants.

trans-unit.source: This was an open-label, dose-escalating study to assess safety, tolerability, PK, PD, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adults with HPP.
trans-unit.target: This was an open-label, dose-escalating study to assess safety, tolerability, PK, PD, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adults with HPP.

trans-unit.source: Study participants were enrolled into 3 cohorts (ALXN1850 15 mg, 45 mg, and 90 mg) in a sequential fashion. An SRC convened after the first participant in each cohort had completed the first IV dose to assess safety and tolerability, and to endorse initiation of SC study drug administration to the first participant as well as IV and SC study drug administration to the remaining participants in the cohort. Once at least 3 participants in each of the first 2 cohorts
trans-unit.target: Study participants were enrolled into 3 cohorts (ALXN1850 15 mg, 45 mg, and 90 mg) in a sequential fashion. An SRC convened after the first participant in each cohort had completed the first IV dose to assess safety and tolerability, and to endorse initiation of SC study drug administration to the first participant as well as IV and SC study drug administration to the remaining participants in the cohort. Once at least 3 participants in each of the first 2 cohorts

trans-unit.source: (15 mg and 45 mg) had received a single IV dose and 3 qw SC doses, the SRC convened again to review safety and tolerability data, and to determine if the final dose escalation (90 mg) could proceed. After the SRC endorsed each dose escalation, the next higher cohort began dosing at the next dosing level. Cohorts 2 and 3 began dosing after the SRC had reviewed all relevant safety, tolerability, and PK data (for Cohort 3 dose escalation only) from participants in Cohort 1 or Cohorts 1 and 2, respectively, and endorsed dose escalation.
trans-unit.target: (15 mg and 45 mg) had received a single IV dose and 3 qw SC doses, the SRC convened again to review safety and tolerability data, and to determine if the final dose escalation (90 mg) could proceed. After the SRC endorsed each dose escalation, the next higher cohort began dosing at the next dosing level. Cohorts 2 and 3 began dosing after the SRC had reviewed all relevant safety, tolerability, and PK data (for Cohort 3 dose escalation only) from participants in Cohort 1 or Cohorts 1 and 2, respectively, and endorsed dose escalation.

trans-unit.source: Participants who were positive for ADA at the last assessment of the study were given the option to enroll in the Global HPP Registry to enable further monitoring of their ADA values after the end of the study.
trans-unit.target: Participants who were positive for ADA at the last assessment of the study were given the option to enroll in the Global HPP Registry to enable further monitoring of their ADA values after the end of the study.

trans-unit.source: Approximately 15 eligible participants (approximately 5 in each Cohorts 1, 2, and 3), were planned to be enrolled to allow for at least 9 participants (3 per cohort) to “complete” the study. A total of 23 participants were screened and provided informed consent, and 15 participants were dosed and included in the safety, PK, PD, and immunogenicity analysis sets.
trans-unit.target: Approximately 15 eligible participants (approximately 5 in each Cohorts 1, 2, and 3), were planned to be enrolled to allow for at least 9 participants (3 per cohort) to “complete” the study. A total of 23 participants were screened and provided informed consent, and 15 participants were dosed and included in the safety, PK, PD, and immunogenicity analysis sets.

trans-unit.source: Diagnosis and Main Criteria for Inclusion and Exclusion:
trans-unit.target: Diagnosis and Main Criteria for Inclusion and Exclusion:

trans-unit.source: Male and nonpregnant female participants at least 18 years of age with a clinical diagnosis of HPP and in otherwise good general health. All participants provided written informed consent.
trans-unit.target: Male and nonpregnant female participants at least 18 years of age with a clinical diagnosis of HPP and in otherwise good general health. All participants provided written informed consent.

trans-unit.source: Study Interventions, Dose, Mode of Administration, and Lot Number(s): ALXN1850 SC qw over 3 weeks (a total of 3 doses) at 15, 45, or 90 mg ALXN1850 IV single dose at 15, 45, or 90 mg
trans-unit.target: Study Interventions, Dose, Mode of Administration, and Lot Number(s): ALXN1850 SC qw over 3 weeks (a total of 3 doses) at 15, 45, or 90 mg ALXN1850 IV single dose at 15, 45, or 90 mg

trans-unit.source: The planned study duration for each participant was up to 22.5 weeks (approximately 5 months) in total. The first participant in each cohort had a duration of up to 155 days (approximately
trans-unit.target: The planned study duration for each participant was up to 22.5 weeks (approximately 5 months) in total. The first participant in each cohort had a duration of up to 155 days (approximately

trans-unit.source: 5 months). The screening period was up to 56 days, the treatment period was 65 days, and the follow-up period was up to 34 days. Subsequent participants in each cohort had a duration of 148 days (approximately 5 months). The screening period was up to 56 days, the treatment period was 57 days, and the follow-up period was up to 35 days.
trans-unit.target: 5 months). The screening period was up to 56 days, the treatment period was 65 days, and the follow-up period was up to 34 days. Subsequent participants in each cohort had a duration of 148 days (approximately 5 months). The screening period was up to 56 days, the treatment period was 57 days, and the follow-up period was up to 35 days.

trans-unit.source: There were 4 male participants (26.7%) and 11 female participants (73.3%) dosed in the study. The median age was 47.0 years (range 31 to 57 years). All 15 participants were White and not Hispanic or Latino.
trans-unit.target: There were 4 male participants (26.7%) and 11 female participants (73.3%) dosed in the study. The median age was 47.0 years (range 31 to 57 years). All 15 participants were White and not Hispanic or Latino.

trans-unit.source: All participants received one IV dose and 3 SC doses with the exception of 1 participant in Cohort 2 that received only 1 of 3 SC doses and 2 participants in Cohort 3 that received 2 of 3 SC doses.
trans-unit.target: All participants received one IV dose and 3 SC doses with the exception of 1 participant in Cohort 2 that received only 1 of 3 SC doses and 2 participants in Cohort 3 that received 2 of 3 SC doses.

trans-unit.source: Listed below are the objectives and endpoints that are described in this report.
trans-unit.target: Listed below are the objectives and endpoints that are described in this report.

trans-unit.source: Assess the safety and tolerability of ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses
trans-unit.target: Assess the safety and tolerability of ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses

trans-unit.source: Number and proportion of participants with at least one TEAE or TESAE.
trans-unit.target: Number and proportion of participants with at least one TEAE or TESAE.

trans-unit.source: Overall, 12 participants (80.0%) experienced any TEAE during the study; 9 participants (60.0%) experienced 13 TEAEs after IV dosing and 12 participants (80.0%) experienced 33 TEAEs after SC dosing. TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1,
trans-unit.target: Overall, 12 participants (80.0%) experienced any TEAE during the study; 9 participants (60.0%) experienced 13 TEAEs after IV dosing and 12 participants (80.0%) experienced 33 TEAEs after SC dosing. TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1,

trans-unit.source: Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.
trans-unit.target: Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.

trans-unit.source: No deaths were reported during the study. One participant (6.7%) from Cohort 3 experienced a TESAE (atrial fibrillation) which was considered by the investigator as not related to study drug. No participants experienced TEAEs leading to withdrawal of study drug.
trans-unit.target: No deaths were reported during the study. One participant (6.7%) from Cohort 3 experienced a TESAE (atrial fibrillation) which was considered by the investigator as not related to study drug. No participants experienced TEAEs leading to withdrawal of study drug.

trans-unit.source: A total of 10 participants (66.7%) experienced TEAEs considered related to ALXN1850.
trans-unit.target: A total of 10 participants (66.7%) experienced TEAEs considered related to ALXN1850.

trans-unit.target: Treatment-related TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in

trans-unit.source: Assess the PK of single IV and 3 SC doses of ALXN1850
trans-unit.target: Assess the PK of single IV and 3 SC doses of ALXN1850

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 PK (activity) over time profiles and PK parameters
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 PK (activity) over time profiles and PK parameters

trans-unit.source: Dose proportionality of plasma ALXN1850 PK parameters over the dose range tested. A power model was fitted to describe the relationship between Y (Cmax, AUC168, AUCinf, and AUCtau) and X (dose) using the least squares linear regression model. Dose proportionality was also analyzed based on ADA status.
trans-unit.target: Dose proportionality of plasma ALXN1850 PK parameters over the dose range tested. A power model was fitted to describe the relationship between Y (Cmax, AUC168, AUCinf, and AUCtau) and X (dose) using the least squares linear regression model. Dose proportionality was also analyzed based on ADA status.

trans-unit.source: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 for all participants increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg.
trans-unit.target: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 for all participants increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg.

trans-unit.source: 48 hours for post IV and SC administration, respectively.

trans-unit.source: For all participants in cohorts following IV dose, mean clearance was 0.04 L/h and the mean volume of distribution was 4.17 L.
trans-unit.target: For all participants in cohorts following IV dose, mean clearance was 0.04 L/h and the mean volume of distribution was 4.17 L.

trans-unit.source: Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was between 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively.
trans-unit.target: Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was between 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively.

trans-unit.source: For all participants in cohorts following SC dose 3, apparent mean clearnace was 0.16 L/h and the apparent mean volume of distribution was 19.3 L.

trans-unit.source: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC administration was assessed by comparing AUCtau values of the first SC dose versus AUCinf of the IV dose for the similar dose cohorts.
trans-unit.target: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC administration was assessed by comparing AUCtau values of the first SC dose versus AUCinf of the IV dose for the similar dose cohorts.

trans-unit.source: Log transformed AUCtau and AUCinf estimates were analyzed using an analysis of variance model with group (IV vs SC) as a factor. The parameter estimates results were exponentiated and the ratio of geometric means and 90% CIs were presented. Analysis was repeated by

trans-unit.source: A total of 4 (26.7%) participants had ADA positive status, including
trans-unit.target: A total of 4 (26.7%) participants had ADA positive status, including

trans-unit.source: immunogenicity and 1 (6.7%) with treatment-emergent ADA response. All ADA positive participants exhibited low titers (range: 10 to 80). No participants had NAb positive results. Treatment-emergent ADA positive response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated AE.
trans-unit.target: immunogenicity and 1 (6.7%) with treatment-emergent ADA response. All ADA positive participants exhibited low titers (range: 10 to 80). No participants had NAb positive results. Treatment-emergent ADA positive response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated AE.

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses had an acceptable safety profile and was well tolerated in adults with HPP.
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses had an acceptable safety profile and was well tolerated in adults with HPP.

trans-unit.source: No deaths were reported during the study.
trans-unit.target: No deaths were reported during the study.

trans-unit.source: Injection site reactions (10 occurrences) were reported for 8 participants (53.3%) after receiving ALXN1850 via SC injection. No adverse events of hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reaction were reported during the study.
trans-unit.target: Injection site reactions (10 occurrences) were reported for 8 participants (53.3%) after receiving ALXN1850 via SC injection. No adverse events of hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reaction were reported during the study.

trans-unit.source: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg. Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively. Statistical analysis of trough concentrations demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8.
trans-unit.target: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg. Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively. Statistical analysis of trough concentrations demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8.

trans-unit.source: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC dose ranged from 28.6% to 36.7%.
trans-unit.target: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC dose ranged from 28.6% to 36.7%.

trans-unit.source: The concentrations of PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio decreased following IV dose and over time returned to baseline levels. The magnitude and duration of ALXN1850 induced reductions in PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio appear dose dependent. The maximum lowering effects occurred after IV dose in 7 days, and lowering effects were sustained for 3 to 4 weeks post last SC dose prior to returning to baseline.
trans-unit.target: The concentrations of PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio decreased following IV dose and over time returned to baseline levels. The magnitude and duration of ALXN1850 induced reductions in PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio appear dose dependent. The maximum lowering effects occurred after IV dose in 7 days, and lowering effects were sustained for 3 to 4 weeks post last SC dose prior to returning to baseline.

trans-unit.source: Treatment-emergent positive ADA response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated adverse event.
trans-unit.target: Treatment-emergent positive ADA response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated adverse event.

trans-unit.source: Hypophosphatasia is a rare, inherited, metabolic disease caused by deficient activity of the TNSALP, typically due to loss of function mutation(s) in the ALPL gene. As a direct result of deficient TNSALP, progressive damage to vital organs along with other clinical sequelae may occur, including deformity and softness of bones (rickets-like bone deformities and osteomalacia), fractures and pseudofractures, pain, profound muscle weakness, respiratory failure (primarily in infants), seizures (mainly in infants), impaired renal function, impaired mobility, and dental abnormalities (Berkseth et al 2013; Rockman-Greenberg 2013; Weber et al 2016; Whyte et al 2013). Asfotase alfa (STRENSIQ®) is currently the only approved treatment for patients with pediatric-onset HPP.
trans-unit.target: Hypophosphatasia is a rare, inherited, metabolic disease caused by deficient activity of the TNSALP, typically due to loss of function mutation(s) in the ALPL gene. As a direct result of deficient TNSALP, progressive damage to vital organs along with other clinical sequelae may occur, including deformity and softness of bones (rickets-like bone deformities and osteomalacia), fractures and pseudofractures, pain, profound muscle weakness, respiratory failure (primarily in infants), seizures (mainly in infants), impaired renal function, impaired mobility, and dental abnormalities (Berkseth et al 2013; Rockman-Greenberg 2013; Weber et al 2016; Whyte et al 2013). Asfotase alfa (STRENSIQ®) is currently the only approved treatment for patients with pediatric-onset HPP.

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 is an investigational ERT that addresses the underlying cause of HPP by replacing the defective enzyme, thus reversing the mineralization defects of the skeleton and improving systemic manifestations of HPP. ALXN1850 is a human recombinant
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 is an investigational ERT that addresses the underlying cause of HPP by replacing the defective enzyme, thus reversing the mineralization defects of the skeleton and improving systemic manifestations of HPP. ALXN1850 is a human recombinant

trans-unit.source: TNSALP-Fc-deca-aspartate fusion protein. It is a soluble glycoprotein comprised of
trans-unit.target: TNSALP-Fc-deca-aspartate fusion protein. It is a soluble glycoprotein comprised of

trans-unit.source: 2 polypeptide chains of 724 amino acids made from the catalytic domain of human TNSALP (SwissProt, P05186), the human Ig G2/4 Fc domain (SwissProt P01859 and Genebank K01316; to facilitate purification and extend half-life), and a deca aspartate peptide (to target the bone).
trans-unit.target: 2 polypeptide chains of 724 amino acids made from the catalytic domain of human TNSALP (SwissProt, P05186), the human Ig G2/4 Fc domain (SwissProt P01859 and Genebank K01316; to facilitate purification and extend half-life), and a deca aspartate peptide (to target the bone).

trans-unit.source: The purpose of this FIH, open-label, dose-escalating study was to assess the safety and tolerability, PK and PD parameters, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adult participants with HPP. This FIH study was designed to support an efficient and tolerable pharmacologically effective dose in participants with HPP for whom ALXN1850 has been designed to deliver the required enzyme with less frequent SC administration than asfotase alfa, and therefore reduce the burden of treatment for this group of patients.
trans-unit.target: The purpose of this FIH, open-label, dose-escalating study was to assess the safety and tolerability, PK and PD parameters, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adult participants with HPP. This FIH study was designed to support an efficient and tolerable pharmacologically effective dose in participants with HPP for whom ALXN1850 has been designed to deliver the required enzyme with less frequent SC administration than asfotase alfa, and therefore reduce the burden of treatment for this group of patients.

trans-unit.source: Assess the safety and tolerability of ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses

trans-unit.source: This was an open-label, dose-escalating study to assess safety, tolerability, PK, PD, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adults with HPP.
trans-unit.target: This was an open-label, dose-escalating study to assess safety, tolerability, PK, PD, and immunogenicity of ALXN1850 when given IV and SC to adults with HPP.

trans-unit.source: Study participants were enrolled into 3 cohorts (ALXN1850 15 mg, 45 mg, and 90 mg) in a sequential fashion. An SRC convened after the first participant in each cohort had completed the first IV dose to assess safety and tolerability, and to endorse initiation of SC study drug administration to the first participant as well as IV and SC study drug administration to the remaining participants in the cohort. Once at least 3 participants in each of the first 2 cohorts
trans-unit.target: Study participants were enrolled into 3 cohorts (ALXN1850 15 mg, 45 mg, and 90 mg) in a sequential fashion. An SRC convened after the first participant in each cohort had completed the first IV dose to assess safety and tolerability, and to endorse initiation of SC study drug administration to the first participant as well as IV and SC study drug administration to the remaining participants in the cohort. Once at least 3 participants in each of the first 2 cohorts

trans-unit.source: (15 mg and 45 mg) had received a single IV dose and 3 qw SC doses, the SRC convened again to review safety and tolerability data, and to determine if the final dose escalation (90 mg) could proceed. After the SRC endorsed each dose escalation, the next higher cohort began dosing at the next dosing level. Cohorts 2 and 3 began dosing after the SRC had reviewed all relevant safety, tolerability, and PK data (for Cohort 3 dose escalation only) from participants in Cohort 1 or Cohorts 1 and 2, respectively, and endorsed dose escalation.
trans-unit.target: (15 mg and 45 mg) had received a single IV dose and 3 qw SC doses, the SRC convened again to review safety and tolerability data, and to determine if the final dose escalation (90 mg) could proceed. After the SRC endorsed each dose escalation, the next higher cohort began dosing at the next dosing level. Cohorts 2 and 3 began dosing after the SRC had reviewed all relevant safety, tolerability, and PK data (for Cohort 3 dose escalation only) from participants in Cohort 1 or Cohorts 1 and 2, respectively, and endorsed dose escalation.

trans-unit.source: Participants who were positive for ADA at the last assessment of the study were given the option to enroll in the Global HPP Registry to enable further monitoring of their ADA values after the end of the study.
trans-unit.target: Participants who were positive for ADA at the last assessment of the study were given the option to enroll in the Global HPP Registry to enable further monitoring of their ADA values after the end of the study.

trans-unit.source: As this was the first time ALXN1850 was dosed in humans, an open-label dose-escalating design was chosen for initial assessment of the safety, tolerability, PK, and PD of ALXN1850 IV and SC in adults with HPP. The scientific rationale for each aspect of the study design included the following:
trans-unit.target: As this was the first time ALXN1850 was dosed in humans, an open-label dose-escalating design was chosen for initial assessment of the safety, tolerability, PK, and PD of ALXN1850 IV and SC in adults with HPP. The scientific rationale for each aspect of the study design included the following:

trans-unit.source: This study was conducted in adult participants with HPP because ALXN1850 was designed as a TNSALP ERT that was expected to lead to improvement in this patient population. Because of the potential for immunogenicity, this study enrolled adults with HPP who were not anticipated to require continued treatment with ERT, based on their signs and symptoms, and the investigator’s clinical assessment of their current condition.
trans-unit.target: This study was conducted in adult participants with HPP because ALXN1850 was designed as a TNSALP ERT that was expected to lead to improvement in this patient population. Because of the potential for immunogenicity, this study enrolled adults with HPP who were not anticipated to require continued treatment with ERT, based on their signs and symptoms, and the investigator’s clinical assessment of their current condition.

trans-unit.source: A single ALXN1850 IV dose provided the reference necessary for assessing absolute bioavailability.
trans-unit.target: A single ALXN1850 IV dose provided the reference necessary for assessing absolute bioavailability.

trans-unit.source: A weekly SC dosing provided the PK and PD information that supported the intended dose regimen in this patient population.
trans-unit.target: A weekly SC dosing provided the PK and PD information that supported the intended dose regimen in this patient population.

trans-unit.source: Three weekly doses of SC ALXN1850 provided accumulation information for this study drug.
trans-unit.target: Three weekly doses of SC ALXN1850 provided accumulation information for this study drug.

trans-unit.source: The qw SC dose range proposed for this study (15 to 90 mg) was expected to cover the therapeutic range in humans.
trans-unit.target: The qw SC dose range proposed for this study (15 to 90 mg) was expected to cover the therapeutic range in humans.

trans-unit.source: An 8-week follow-up provided enough time to assess immunogenicity and possible risks to the participants.
trans-unit.target: An 8-week follow-up provided enough time to assess immunogenicity and possible risks to the participants.

trans-unit.source: PPD, Inc. served as the CRO for this study and was responsible for project management, clinical monitoring, data management, biostatistics and statistical programming, PK, PD, immunogenicity analysis, and final reporting. PPD was also used as the central laboratory.

trans-unit.source: Enrolled in this study were male and nonpregnant female participants at least 18 years of age with a clinical diagnosis of HPP and in otherwise good general health. All participants provided written informed consent. Detailed inclusion and exclusion criteria are provided in protocol Section 5.1 and protocol Section 5.2 (Appendix 16.1.1 Protocol or Amendment).
trans-unit.target: Enrolled in this study were male and nonpregnant female participants at least 18 years of age with a clinical diagnosis of HPP and in otherwise good general health. All participants provided written informed consent. Detailed inclusion and exclusion criteria are provided in protocol Section 5.1 and protocol Section 5.2 (Appendix 16.1.1 Protocol or Amendment).

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 was formulated at pH 7.3 and each vial contained 100 mg of ALXN1850 in 10 mM sodium phosphate, 140 mM proline, 140 mM sucrose, and 0.05% w/v polysorbate
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 was formulated at pH 7.3 and each vial contained 100 mg of ALXN1850 in 10 mM sodium phosphate, 140 mM proline, 140 mM sucrose, and 0.05% w/v polysorbate

trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 was formulated at pH 7.3 and each vial contained 100 mg of ALXN1850 in 10 mM sodium phosphate, 140 mM proline, 140 mM

trans-unit.target: sucrose, and 0.05% w/v polysorbate 80. The concentration was 100 mg/mL.

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 was administered via the SC route qw over 3 weeks (a total of 3 doses) at 15, 45, or 90 mg.
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 was administered via the SC route qw over 3 weeks (a total of 3 doses) at 15, 45, or 90 mg.

trans-unit.source: SC doses were administered as a manual SC injection in either the abdomen or thigh at the investigator’s discretion.
trans-unit.target: SC doses were administered as a manual SC injection in either the abdomen or thigh at the investigator’s discretion.

trans-unit.source: A single dose of ALXN1850 at 15, 45, or 90 mg was administered as an IV infusion per cohort.
trans-unit.target: A single dose of ALXN1850 at 15, 45, or 90 mg was administered as an IV infusion per cohort.

trans-unit.source: Study centers were monitored by the CRO. Centers were visited at regular intervals and a Visit Log was maintained. Monitors were responsible for reviewing adherence to the protocol; compliance with GCP; and the completeness, accuracy, and consistency of the data. Direct access to participant medical and laboratory records was permitted to verify entries on the study-specific CRFs.
trans-unit.target: Study centers were monitored by the CRO. Centers were visited at regular intervals and a Visit Log was maintained. Monitors were responsible for reviewing adherence to the protocol; compliance with GCP; and the completeness, accuracy, and consistency of the data. Direct access to participant medical and laboratory records was permitted to verify entries on the study-specific CRFs.

trans-unit.source: Investigator staff training was provided by the CRO during study initiation and routine monitoring visits. The Sponsor organized investigator and clinical research associate meetings before study start and during the study to provide information on the investigational product, the study rationale and design, responsibilities under ICH, FDA, and GCP guidelines, and training on the detailed study requirements.
trans-unit.target: Investigator staff training was provided by the CRO during study initiation and routine monitoring visits. The Sponsor organized investigator and clinical research associate meetings before study start and during the study to provide information on the investigational product, the study rationale and design, responsibilities under ICH, FDA, and GCP guidelines, and training on the detailed study requirements.

trans-unit.source: Given that the primary endpoint of this study is safety and tolerability, all participants who completed end of treatment assessments are considered to have completed the study. This is a change from the definition in the protocol where a participant would be defined as “complete” if all PK/PD sampling was performed within window, all doses were received per the Schedule of Activities, and end of treatment assessments were completed.
trans-unit.target: Given that the primary endpoint of this study is safety and tolerability, all participants who completed end of treatment assessments are considered to have completed the study. This is a change from the definition in the protocol where a participant would be defined as “complete” if all PK/PD sampling was performed within window, all doses were received per the Schedule of Activities, and end of treatment assessments were completed.

trans-unit.source: All 15 participants (100%) reported taking at least 1 concomitant medication (Table 14.1.4.1 and Listing 16.2.4.3). Concomitant medications were taken to treat TEAEs as follows: ibuprofen for headache, Nyquil (dextromethorphan hydrobromide, doxylamine succinate, ephedrine sulfate, ethanol, paracetamol) for nasal congestion, sulfamethoxazole/trimethoprim for urinary tract infection, miconazole nitrate for vaginal yeast infection, ascorbic acid and zinc for coronavirus, and acetylsalicylic acid for atrial fibrillation.

trans-unit.source: Adverse events are summarized overall and by cohort as well as by treatment; however, the number of participants was small and therefore comparisons between dose levels and treatments must be made with caution. Number of participants (N) is too small to draw conclusions.
trans-unit.target: Adverse events are summarized overall and by cohort as well as by treatment; however, the number of participants was small and therefore comparisons between dose levels and treatments must be made with caution. Number of participants (N) is too small to draw conclusions.

trans-unit.source: Overall, 12 participants (80.0%) experienced any TEAE during the study; 9 participants (60.0%) experienced 13 TEAEs after IV dosing and 12 participants (80.0%) experienced 33 TEAEs after SC dosing. TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1, 5 participants (100%) in Cohort 2, and 4 participants (80.0%) in Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.
trans-unit.target: Overall, 12 participants (80.0%) experienced any TEAE during the study; 9 participants (60.0%) experienced 13 TEAEs after IV dosing and 12 participants (80.0%) experienced 33 TEAEs after SC dosing. TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1, 5 participants (100%) in Cohort 2, and 4 participants (80.0%) in Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.

trans-unit.source: No deaths were reported during the study. One participant (6.7%) from Cohort 3 experienced a TESAE (atrial fibrillation) which was considered by the investigator as not related to study drug; a narrative for the event is presented in Section 5.1.1.4. No participants experienced TEAEs leading to withdrawal of study drug.
trans-unit.target: No deaths were reported during the study. One participant (6.7%) from Cohort 3 experienced a TESAE (atrial fibrillation) which was considered by the investigator as not related to study drug; a narrative for the event is presented in Section 5.1.1.4. No participants experienced TEAEs leading to withdrawal of study drug.

trans-unit.source: A total of 10 participants (66.7%) experienced TEAEs considered related to ALXN1850. Treatment-related TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1, 5 participants
trans-unit.target: A total of 10 participants (66.7%) experienced TEAEs considered related to ALXN1850. Treatment-related TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1, 5 participants

trans-unit.source: (100%) in Cohort 2, and 2 participants (40.0%) in Cohort 3. A participant with multiple occurrences of a TEAE is counted only once at the highest relationship.
trans-unit.target: (100%) in Cohort 2, and 2 participants (40.0%) in Cohort 3. A participant with multiple occurrences of a TEAE is counted only once at the highest relationship.

trans-unit.source: Site reactions occurred with 10 SC injection administrations of ALXN1850 for 8 participants with a total of 13 events.
trans-unit.target: Site reactions occurred with 10 SC injection administrations of ALXN1850 for 8 participants with a total of 13 events.

trans-unit.source: Five participants reported a total of 9 AEs, and an additional 3 participants had reactions captured on the injection/infusion site evaluation form that were not reported as AEs. In addition, 1 participant with a reported AE had an additional reaction noted on the injection/infusion site evaluation form that was not reported as an AE.
trans-unit.target: Five participants reported a total of 9 AEs, and an additional 3 participants had reactions captured on the injection/infusion site evaluation form that were not reported as AEs. In addition, 1 participant with a reported AE had an additional reaction noted on the injection/infusion site evaluation form that was not reported as an AE.

trans-unit.source: There was 1 participant who reported an injection site reaction with all 3 SC injections (total of 5 AEs reported); the remaining 7 participants had an injection site reaction only with 1 injection with only 1 participant reporting 2 events at the injection site (total of 8 events of which 4 were reported as AEs).
trans-unit.target: There was 1 participant who reported an injection site reaction with all 3 SC injections (total of 5 AEs reported); the remaining 7 participants had an injection site reaction only with 1 injection with only 1 participant reporting 2 events at the injection site (total of 8 events of which 4 were reported as AEs).

trans-unit.source: Following medical review by the Sponsor, no systemic reactions within the 24-hour period following SC study drug administration. There were no reports consistent with hypersensitivity reactions or anaphylaxis following IV study drug administration. Evaluation of reactions following SC study drug administration is further discussed in Section 5.1.1.6).
trans-unit.target: Following medical review by the Sponsor, no systemic reactions within the 24-hour period following SC study drug administration. There were no reports consistent with hypersensitivity reactions or anaphylaxis following IV study drug administration. Evaluation of reactions following SC study drug administration is further discussed in Section 5.1.1.6).

trans-unit.source: Following IV administration of ALXN1850, mild induration, not reported as an AE, was observed for 1 participant as recorded on the injection/infusion site evaluation form. Following
trans-unit.target: Following IV administration of ALXN1850, mild induration, not reported as an AE, was observed for 1 participant as recorded on the injection/infusion site evaluation form. Following

trans-unit.source: medical review by the Sponsor, 1 participant was identified with mild headache, with onset within 24 hours of study drug infusion, that was considered consistent with a possible infusion associated reaction. There were no other reports consistent with hypersensitivity reactions or anaphylaxis following IV study drug administration. Evaluation of these events is further discussed in Section 5.1.1.6.
trans-unit.target: medical review by the Sponsor, 1 participant was identified with mild headache, with onset within 24 hours of study drug infusion, that was considered consistent with a possible infusion associated reaction. There were no other reports consistent with hypersensitivity reactions or anaphylaxis following IV study drug administration. Evaluation of these events is further discussed in Section 5.1.1.6.

trans-unit.source: Overall, 46 TEAEs were experienced by 12 participants (80.0%). Twelve TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1; 26 TEAEs were reported for 5 participants (100%) in Cohort 2, and 8 TEAEs were reported for 4 participants (80.0%) in Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.
trans-unit.target: Overall, 46 TEAEs were experienced by 12 participants (80.0%). Twelve TEAEs were reported for 3 participants (60.0%) in Cohort 1; 26 TEAEs were reported for 5 participants (100%) in Cohort 2, and 8 TEAEs were reported for 4 participants (80.0%) in Cohort 3. One TEAE in a participant in Cohort 1 was not included in the TEAEs count due to an incomplete AE start date and therefore the event could not be assigned to treatment with IV or SC administration. While the number of participants with events was not affected, the number of TEAEs and related TEAEs for Cohort 1 and overall does not reflect this event.

trans-unit.source: The majority of TEAEs occurred in the SOC of general disorders and administration site conditions. The most frequently reported TEAEs were headache and coronavirus infection (3 participants [20.0%] each), fatigue, injection site bruising, injection site erythema, hyperphosphatemia, anemia, and hematuria (2 participants [13.3%] each).
trans-unit.target: The majority of TEAEs occurred in the SOC of general disorders and administration site conditions. The most frequently reported TEAEs were headache and coronavirus infection (3 participants [20.0%] each), fatigue, injection site bruising, injection site erythema, hyperphosphatemia, anemia, and hematuria (2 participants [13.3%] each).

trans-unit.source: The majority of TEAEs were reported as grade 1 or grade 2 events. One participant (6.7%) in Cohort 2 and 1 participant (6.7%) in Cohort 3 experienced grade 3 TEAEs (hypertension and atrial fibrillation, respectively). There were no grade 4 TEAEs reported.

trans-unit.source: Participant 01500017 (Cohort 3), a 57-year-old White female with hypophosphatasia, received a 90 mg ALXN1850 IV dose on Day 1 and 90 mg ALXN1850 SC dose on Day 8, 15, and 22. The participant had no other relevant medical history.
trans-unit.target: Participant 01500017 (Cohort 3), a 57-year-old White female with hypophosphatasia, received a 90 mg ALXN1850 IV dose on Day 1 and 90 mg ALXN1850 SC dose on Day 8, 15, and 22. The participant had no other relevant medical history.

trans-unit.source: On Day 50, 28 days after the last dose of study drug, the participant experienced a TESAE of grade 3 atrial fibrillation that resulted in hospitalization. It was reported that just before arrival to the emergency room, the participant got out of bed quickly, felt nauseated with sudden syncope, collapsed with severe intensity, and lost consciousness. The participant experienced chills, weakness, and sacral pain. The participant had orthostatic hypotension upon emergency room admission and also had slightly elevated creatinine. On the same day, the participant was hospitalized for observation and an ECG showed atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response. A repeat ECG showed a normal sinus rhythm. An echocardiogram showed normal systolic function, ejection fraction of 60%, trace mitral regurgitation, and trace tricuspid valve regurgitation.
trans-unit.target: On Day 50, 28 days after the last dose of study drug, the participant experienced a TESAE of grade 3 atrial fibrillation that resulted in hospitalization. It was reported that just before arrival to the emergency room, the participant got out of bed quickly, felt nauseated with sudden syncope, collapsed with severe intensity, and lost consciousness. The participant experienced chills, weakness, and sacral pain. The participant had orthostatic hypotension upon emergency room admission and also had slightly elevated creatinine. On the same day, the participant was hospitalized for observation and an ECG showed atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response. A repeat ECG showed a normal sinus rhythm. An echocardiogram showed normal systolic function, ejection fraction of 60%, trace mitral regurgitation, and trace tricuspid valve regurgitation.

trans-unit.source: On Day 51, ECG showed an ejection fraction of 60% with trace mitral regurgitation with no other significant abnormality. The event was considered resolved on Day 51 and the participant was discharged from the hospital. The participant was given acetylsalicylic acid for the event of atrial fibrillation. The investigator considered the TESAE not related to the study drug.
trans-unit.target: On Day 51, ECG showed an ejection fraction of 60% with trace mitral regurgitation with no other significant abnormality. The event was considered resolved on Day 51 and the participant was discharged from the hospital. The participant was given acetylsalicylic acid for the event of atrial fibrillation. The investigator considered the TESAE not related to the study drug.

trans-unit.source: Adverse events of interest included localized injection/infusion site reactions and injection/infusion associated reactions.
trans-unit.target: Adverse events of interest included localized injection/infusion site reactions and injection/infusion associated reactions.

trans-unit.source: Injection/infusion site reactions included AEs localized to the site of study drug administration coded to PTs under the high-level terms (HLTs) of injection site reactions or infusion site reactions, occurring at any time during study participation. Note: The site reactions related to study drug also include events related to the administration of study drug (e.g., injection site bruising). Additionally, clinical signs and symptoms captured on the injection/infusion site evaluation form and not reported separately as AEs are also included in the overall presentation of injection/infusion site reactions.
trans-unit.target: Injection/infusion site reactions included AEs localized to the site of study drug administration coded to PTs under the high-level terms (HLTs) of injection site reactions or infusion site reactions, occurring at any time during study participation. Additionally, clinical signs and symptoms captured on the injection/infusion site evaluation form and not reported separately as AEs are also included in the overall presentation of injection/infusion site reactions.

trans-unit.source: Injection/infusion site reactions reported as AEs are summarized in Table 14.3.1.7 and are presented by participant in Listing 14.3.2.3. Five participants reported 9 TEAEs following SC administration. This includes 1 participant who reported a total of 5 TEAEs for 3 injections. Evaluation of injection/infusion site reactions is presented by participant in Listing 16.2.8.12. Four clinical observations of erythema (Participants 01500006, 01500009, 01500018, and 1500020) following SC administration were captured on the injection/infusion site evaluation form (see Listing 16.2.8.12) but were not reported as AEs. These clinical observations do not appear in the AE summary tables and listing for injection reactions but are included below in the overall presentation of injection site reactions.

trans-unit.target: Cohort 1: Participants received 15 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 15 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 2: Participants received 45 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 45 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 3: Participants received 90 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 90 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses. Source: End-of-Text Figure 14.2.1.1.

trans-unit.source: Following single dose IV administration of ALXN1850 to adults with HPP, the mean plasma concentration versus nominal time profiles for the first participant and subsequent participants in each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose) were stable and followed a similarly parallel trend without clearly discernible distribution phase. Time to attain peak plasma concentration of ALXN1850 (the median estimate) was noted at 2.00, 0.60, and 0.72 hours for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively, relative to the 0.5-hour IV infusion duration. Thereafter, concentrations declined gradually reaching close to below the limit of quantification by 336 hours (the last sample collected) for the first participants while, for the subsequent participants, the last sample after IV infusion was collected at 168 hours without reaching hour-336.
trans-unit.target: Following single dose IV administration of ALXN1850 to adults with HPP, the mean plasma concentration versus nominal time profiles for the first participant and subsequent participants in each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose) were stable and followed a similarly parallel trend without clearly discernible distribution phase. Time to attain peak plasma concentration of ALXN1850 (the median estimate) was noted at 2.00, 0.60, and 0.72 hours for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively, relative to the 0.5-hour IV infusion duration. Thereafter, concentrations declined gradually reaching close to below the limit of quantification by 336 hours (the last sample collected) for the first participants while, for the subsequent participants, the last sample after IV infusion was collected at 168 hours without reaching hour-336.

trans-unit.source: Following 3 SC doses (administered post IV dose) at 1-week intervals, the mean plasma concentration versus nominal time profiles for the first and the subsequent participants in each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose) were also stable and generally followed a similarly parallel trend. Time to attain peak plasma concentration of ALXN1850 (the median estimate) following the SC dose 1 for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3 for all the participants was around 48 hours. The corresponding values following SC doses 2 and 3 were around 24 hours and between 36 and
trans-unit.target: Following 3 SC doses (administered post IV dose) at 1-week intervals, the mean plasma concentration versus nominal time profiles for the first and the subsequent participants in each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose) were also stable and generally followed a similarly parallel trend. Time to attain peak plasma concentration of ALXN1850 (the median estimate) following the SC dose 1 for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3 for all the participants was around 48 hours. The corresponding values following SC doses 2 and 3 were around 24 hours and between 36 and

trans-unit.source: 48 hours, respectively. The last sample after SC dose 1 and SC dose 2 was collected at 168 hours post SC doses and concentrations are much higher than the limit of quantification.
trans-unit.target: 48 hours, respectively. The last sample after SC dose 1 and SC dose 2 was collected at 168 hours post SC doses and concentrations are much higher than the limit of quantification.

trans-unit.source: In each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose), the mean plasma concentration of ALXN1850 versus nominal time (elapsed time from the first dose in days) profiles for the first and the subsequent participants were in general stable and followed a similar trend. It was noted that the mean concentrations increased with increasing dose (i.e., Cohort 3>Cohort 2>Cohort 1). The exclusion of ALXN1850 concentration-time data from those who had missed doses (sensitivity analysis) have shown consistent PK profiles.
trans-unit.target: In each cohort (15, 45, and 90 mg dose), the mean plasma concentration of ALXN1850 versus nominal time (elapsed time from the first dose in days) profiles for the first and the subsequent participants were in general stable and followed a similar trend. It was noted that the mean concentrations increased with increasing dose (i.e., Cohort 3>Cohort 2>Cohort 1). The exclusion of ALXN1850 concentration-time data from those who had missed doses (sensitivity analysis) have shown consistent PK profiles.

trans-unit.source: Following a single IV and 2 SC dose administrations for subsequent participants at 1-week intervals on Days 1, 8, and 15, the mean trough plasma concentrations on Days 8, 15, and 22 noted were similar for each cohort indicating attainment of steady state. A box plot of trough plasma concentration of ALXN1850 following IV and 2 SC dose administration by cohort also shows that steady state has been achieved.
trans-unit.target: Following a single IV and 2 SC dose administrations for subsequent participants at 1-week intervals on Days 1, 8, and 15, the mean trough plasma concentrations on Days 8, 15, and 22 noted were similar for each cohort indicating attainment of steady state. A box plot of trough plasma concentration of ALXN1850 following IV and 2 SC dose administration by cohort also shows that steady state has been achieved.

trans-unit.source: Plasma PK parameters of ALXN1850 upon administration of IV dose followed by 3 SC doses in adults with HPP are summarized for the IV dose in Table 8, SC dose 1 in Table 9, SC dose 2 in Table 10, and SC dose 3 in Table 11.

trans-unit.source: Individual plasma ALXN1850 concentration versus time by ADA status is presented in Figure 14.2.1.5.
trans-unit.target: Individual plasma ALXN1850 concentration versus time by ADA status is presented in Figure 14.2.1.5.

trans-unit.source: One participant (Cohort 1, 01500003) exhibited treatment-emergent ADA responses on Days 51, 65, and 85, and 3 participants (Cohort 1, 01500004; Cohort 3, 01500019 and 01500020) exhibited pre-existing immunoreactivity; however, no participants tested positive for NAb. The presence of ADA was apparently not associated with any impact on the PK of ALXN1850.
trans-unit.target: One participant (Cohort 1, 01500003) exhibited treatment-emergent ADA responses on Days 51, 65, and 85, and 3 participants (Cohort 1, 01500004; Cohort 3, 01500019 and 01500020) exhibited pre-existing immunoreactivity; however, no participants tested positive for NAb. The presence of ADA was apparently not associated with any impact on the PK of ALXN1850.

trans-unit.source: Following IV and SC doses, in general, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 for all participants (first and subsequent combined) increased with increasing dose from 15 mg to 90 mg.

trans-unit.source: From the study results, ADA apparently did not have impact on the PK parameters of ALXN1850; however, the number of participants that have treatment-emergent ADA response is limited (N = 1) and there was no detectible NAb positivity.
trans-unit.target: From the study results, ADA apparently did not have impact on the PK parameters of ALXN1850; however, the number of participants that have treatment-emergent ADA response is limited (N = 1) and there was no detectible NAb positivity.

trans-unit.source: Following IV dose, for all participants (first and subsequent combined), the arithmetic mean Cmax (peak plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 5.48, 13.8, and 33.5 µg/mL and the arithmetic mean AUCinf (total plasma exposure) was 427, 1030, and 2550 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively. For the first participant, AUCt (total plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 422, 863, and 2030 h*µg/mL and for the subsequent participants, the arithmetic mean AUCt (total plasma exposure) was 339, 851, and 2190 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively.
trans-unit.target: Following IV dose, for all participants (first and subsequent combined), the arithmetic mean Cmax (peak plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 5.48, 13.8, and 33.5 µg/mL and the arithmetic mean AUCinf (total plasma exposure) was 427, 1030, and 2550 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively. For the first participant, AUCt (total plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 422, 863, and 2030 h*µg/mL and for the subsequent participants, the arithmetic mean AUCt (total plasma exposure) was 339, 851, and 2190 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively.

trans-unit.source: Mean clearance for all participants (first and subsequent combined) in cohorts following IV dose was 0.04 L/h and the mean volume of distribution was 4.17 L.
trans-unit.target: Mean clearance for all participants (first and subsequent combined) in cohorts following IV dose was 0.04 L/h and the mean volume of distribution was 4.17 L.

trans-unit.source: Mean half-life of ALXN1850 in all participants (first and subsequent combined) was 72.6 hours.
trans-unit.target: Mean half-life of ALXN1850 in all participants (first and subsequent combined) was 72.6 hours.

trans-unit.source: Following SC dose 1, for all participants (first and subsequent combined), the arithmetic mean Cmax (peak plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 0.925, 3.28, and 9.12 µg/mL and the arithmetic mean AUCtau (total plasma exposure) was 122, 431, and 1200 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively. For the first participant, AUCt (total plasma exposure) of ALXN1850 was 59.0, 628, and 577 h*µg/mL whereas for the subsequent participants, the arithmetic mean AUCt (total plasma exposure) was 137, 471, and 1200 h*µg/mL for Cohorts 1, 2, and 3, respectively.

trans-unit.source: The power model, ln(parameter) = ln(intercept) + slope*ln(dose), was used to estimate the slope and corresponding 90% confidence interval. Dose proportionality was concluded when the 90% confidence interval of the slope (beta) lies entirely within [(1+ln(0.8)/ln(r),1+ln(1.25)/ln(r))], where r is the dose range (highest dose/lowest dose).
trans-unit.target: The power model, ln(parameter) = ln(intercept) + slope*ln(dose), was used to estimate the slope and corresponding 90% confidence interval. Dose proportionality was concluded when the 90% confidence interval of the slope (beta) lies entirely within [(1+ln(0.8)/ln(r),1+ln(1.25)/ln(r))], where r is the dose range (highest dose/lowest dose).

trans-unit.source: Cohort 1: Participants receive 15 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 15 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 2: Participants receive 45 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 45 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 3: Participants receive 90 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 90 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses. Abbreviation: CI = confidence interval; N = number of participants
trans-unit.target: Cohort 1: Participants receive 15 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 15 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 2: Participants receive 45 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 45 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses; Cohort 3: Participants receive 90 mg as a single IV dose, followed by 90 mg SC at 1 week interval for 3 doses. Abbreviation: CI = confidence interval; N = number of participants

trans-unit.source: Attainment of steady state was assessed via linear mixed models using a random subject effect for assessing the relationship between trough concentrations and time among subsequent participants of each cohort. This approach involved an examination of trough concentrations versus time by conducting linear regression and calculating a 95% CI around the slope estimate, and a first instance where 0 was included in the 95% CI indicating that steady state was achieved. Following IV and SC administration, statistical analysis of trough concentrations on Days 8, 15, and 22 for each cohort demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8 for all three cohorts as zero was included in the 95% CI of the regression slope.
trans-unit.target: Attainment of steady state was assessed via linear mixed models using a random subject effect for assessing the relationship between trough concentrations and time among subsequent participants of each cohort. This approach involved an examination of trough concentrations versus time by conducting linear regression and calculating a 95% CI around the slope estimate, and a first instance where 0 was included in the 95% CI indicating that steady state was achieved. Following IV and SC administration, statistical analysis of trough concentrations on Days 8, 15, and 22 for each cohort demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8 for all three cohorts as zero was included in the 95% CI of the regression slope.

trans-unit.source: Mean (±SD) plasma PLP, PLP/PL ratio, and PPi, versus time profiles by cohort (cohorts overlaid) are presented in Figure 9, Figure 10, and Figure 11, respectively. For PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL endpoints, maximum PD-lowering effects occurred after IV infusion in 7 days and returned to < 50% baseline levels prior to start of SC administration. Individual (as well as mean) plasma concentrations decreased after each SC administration and PD-lowering effects were sustained for 3 to 4 weeks after the last SC dose prior to gradually returning to baseline by the end of study on Day 78 or 8 weeks. The magnitude and duration of ALXN1850 induced reductions in PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL appear dose dependent. Compare to PLP mean profiles, PLP/PL ratio mean profiles show apparent lower variability. Note, for the PPi PD endpoint, about 40% of sample concentrations were BLQ (0.75 µM) and the impact of BLQ samples were

trans-unit.source: A summary of ALXN1850 ADA status and NAb response is presented in Table 14.3.4.8.1 and ADA titer by relative time to dose is presented in Table 14.3.4.8.2. Mean ADA titer versus relative time to dose and individual ADA titer versus relative time to dose are presented by cohort in Figure 14.2.3.1 and Figure 14.2.3.2, respectively. A total of 4 (26.7%) participants had ADA positive status, including 3 (20.0%) with pre-existing immunogenicity and 1 (6.7%) with treatment-emergent ADA response. All ADA positive participants exhibited low titers (range: 10 to 80). No participants had NAb positive results. Treatment-emergent positive ADA response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated AE. No participants in the 45 mg and 90 mg dose groups exhibited treatment-emergent ADA responses, suggesting higher doses may result in lower incidence of immunogenicity.
trans-unit.target: A summary of ALXN1850 ADA status and NAb response is presented in Table 14.3.4.8.1 and ADA titer by relative time to dose is presented in Table 14.3.4.8.2. All ADA positive participants exhibited low titers (range: 10 to 80). No participants had NAb positive results. Treatment-emergent positive ADA response did not have apparent impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated AE. No participants in the 45 mg and 90 mg dose groups exhibited treatment-emergent ADA responses, suggesting higher doses may result in lower incidence of immunogenicity.

trans-unit.source: Overall, no clinically meaningful trends in change from baseline over time were noted for the exploratory biomarkers. As discussed in Section 5.1.2.3, hyperphosphatemia was reported for 2 participants (both in Cohort 1) and blood phosphorus increased for 1 participant (Cohort 3);

trans-unit.source: ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses had an acceptable safety profile and was well tolerated in adults with HPP.
trans-unit.target: ALXN1850 given IV as a single dose and given SC in 3 qw doses had an acceptable safety profile and was well tolerated in adults with HPP.

trans-unit.source: No deaths were reported during the study.
trans-unit.target: No deaths were reported during the study.

trans-unit.source: Injection site reactions (10 occurrences) were reported for 8 participants (53.3%) after receiving ALXN1850 via SC injection. No adverse events of hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reaction were reported during the study.
trans-unit.target: Injection site reactions (10 occurrences) were reported for 8 participants (53.3%) after receiving ALXN1850 via SC injection. No adverse events of hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reaction were reported during the study.

trans-unit.source: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg. Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively. Statistical analysis of trough concentrations demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8.
trans-unit.target: Following IV and SC doses, the peak and total exposure of ALXN1850 increased with increasing dose in a greater than dose proportional manner over the dose range of 15 to 90 mg. Mean half-life of ALXN1850 was 72.6 hours and 140 hours following IV and SC administration, respectively. Statistical analysis of trough concentrations demonstrated attainment of steady state on Day 8.

trans-unit.source: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC dose ranged from 28.6% to 36.7%.
trans-unit.target: Absolute bioavailability of ALXN1850 following SC dose ranged from 28.6% to 36.7%.

trans-unit.source: The concentrations of PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio decreased following IV dose and over time returned to baseline levels. The magnitude and duration of ALXN1850 induced reductions in PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio appear dose dependent. The maximum lowering effects occurred after IV dose in 7 days, and lowering effects were sustained for 3 to 4 weeks post last SC dose prior to returning to baseline.
trans-unit.target: The concentrations of PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio decreased following IV dose and over time returned to baseline levels. The magnitude and duration of ALXN1850 induced reductions in PPi, PLP, and PLP/PL ratio appear dose dependent. The maximum lowering effects occurred after IV dose in 7 days, and lowering effects were sustained for 3 to 4 weeks post last SC dose prior to returning to baseline.

trans-unit.source: Treatment-emergent positive ADA response did not have apparent any impact on ALXN1850 PK or PD, and were not associated with hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or any other immune mediated adverse event.
